description: Synthetic communication moments for an EmpathyAI Azure avatar demonstration.

records.title: Naming fear before explaining next steps
records.moment: The patient says she is scared about what happens next and whether she will suffer.
records.learnerApproach: The learner first reflects the fear, validates the need for a clear answer, and asks which question feels most urgent.
records.personaReaction: The patient remains sad but becomes more open because the fear was named before information was offered.
records.coachingInsight: Reflect the specific concern before moving to explanation or process.

records.title: Generic reassurance leaves the concern unanswered
records.moment: The patient asks whether she will be in pain and the learner responds only that the team will take good care of her.
records.learnerApproach: The learner is polite but gives generic reassurance without acknowledging fear or answering the active concern.
records.personaReaction: The patient stays guarded and asks again for a direct, plain-language answer.
records.coachingInsight: Warmth does not replace responsiveness; acknowledge the emotion and address the question directly.

records.title: Repairing prognosis and palliative jargon
records.moment: The learner uses prognosis and palliative pathway without explanation while the patient is processing difficult news.
records.learnerApproach: The learner notices the confusion, apologizes for the clinical wording, explains one idea at a time, and checks what the patient heard.
records.personaReaction: The patient asks a clearer follow-up question after the repair and understanding check.
records.coachingInsight: Repair unexplained jargon immediately and pair plain language with an understanding check.

records.title: Acknowledge the family's protective concern
records.moment: A family member says everyone is talking around the family and asks whether the team is listening to the parent's wishes.
records.learnerApproach: The learner names the frustration, recognizes the protective intent, and asks what the parent said mattered most.
records.personaReaction: The family member becomes less defensive because the question about the parent's wishes is taken seriously.
records.coachingInsight: Recognize both the emotion and the value the family member is trying to protect.

records.title: Process language increases frustration
records.moment: The family member asks for a clear answer and receives a vague description of meetings, teams, and next steps.
records.learnerApproach: The learner initially hides behind process language, then repairs by stating what can be answered now and what still requires discussion.
records.personaReaction: The family member stays frustrated during the vague answer but settles when uncertainty is named honestly.
records.coachingInsight: Do not substitute process for an answer; distinguish known information from genuine uncertainty.

records.title: Check understanding without taking sides
records.moment: Relatives disagree about the direction of care and the family member pressures the learner to declare who is right.
records.learnerApproach: The learner does not arbitrate the family dispute, reflects the shared concern, and checks how the family understands the parent's expressed wishes.
records.personaReaction: The family member remains concerned but shifts from accusation toward clarification.
records.coachingInsight: Stay with communication and understanding rather than deciding the clinical or family dispute.

records.title: Make it safe to ask again
records.moment: The patient admits she nodded even though she did not understand and feels embarrassed asking for another explanation.
records.learnerApproach: The learner removes blame, thanks the patient for saying so, and restarts with one short plain-language idea.
records.personaReaction: The patient becomes less guarded and is willing to identify the part that remains confusing.
records.coachingInsight: Normalize clarification and make understanding a shared responsibility.

records.title: Explain one clinical term at a time
records.moment: The learner uses comorbidity, differential, and clinical pathway in one explanation.
records.learnerApproach: The learner repairs by removing unnecessary terms, defining the one term that must remain, and pausing before the next idea.
records.personaReaction: The patient can repeat the main point in her own words and asks a focused follow-up question.
records.coachingInsight: Reduce cognitive load by explaining one necessary term at a time.

records.title: Own the communication repair
records.moment: The patient says the previous explanation made her feel talked down to.
records.learnerApproach: The learner acknowledges the impact without defending intent, apologizes briefly, and asks permission to try again more clearly.
records.personaReaction: The patient moves from frustration toward cautious relief after the learner owns the repair.
records.coachingInsight: Acknowledge impact before explaining intent; keep the apology short and make the next attempt concrete.
